title: Step 1: Choose  for your desired cluster.

sections.heading: Access Command Line Tools
sections.body:
To access the **Command Line Tools** option in the **Cluster** sidebar:

Click the name of the cluster to open the Cluster sidebar, then click
Command Line Tools.

sections.heading: Connect with mongorestore
sections.body:
> **Note:** Since Atlas doesn't offer an individual `restore` role,
> privilege, or privilege action, you must have the `Atlas admin`
> role to use `mongorestore`.

The Binary Import and Export Tools section of the Command Line Tools tab
displays a copyable template with the minimum required options for
connecting [mongorestore reference page](database-tools/mongorestore) to your Atlas cluster.
For instructions on finding the Command Line Tools tab,
see [Access Command Line Tools](view-command-line-tools).

The template includes placeholder values for certain options.
You must replace these placeholders with the appropriate values for
your Atlas cluster:

- `<PASSWORD>` - replace this with the password for the user
  specified in `--username`. The template includes a database user
  for the project as the `--username`. If you want to authenticate
  as a different user, replace the value of `--username` and
  specify the password for that user in `--password`.

  If your password contains special characters, surround it in double
  or single quotes. For example, if your password is `@bc123`, you
  must surround it in quotes, such as `"@bc123"`.

Include any other [mongorestore reference page](database-tools/mongorestore) command line options as required
before copying and pasting the command into your system terminal and
executing the full command. See [mongorestore reference page](database-tools/mongorestore) for complete
documentation on the available command line options and how to use
them.

> **Note:** 
>
> Connecting to an 
>
> Atlas
>
>  cluster from Microsoft Windows
>
> For users on Windows (Microsoft Windows), specify
> `mongorestore.exe`
>
> You may need to provide the full file
> path to the `mongorestore.exe`.

sections.heading: Connect with mongodump
sections.body:
The Binary Import and Export section of the Command Line Tools tab
displays a copyable template with the minimum required options for
connecting [mongodump reference page](database-tools/mongodump) to your Atlas cluster.
For instructions on finding the Command Line Tools tab,
see [Access Command Line Tools](view-command-line-tools).

The template includes placeholder values for certain options.
You must replace these placeholders with the appropriate values for
your Atlas cluster:

- `<PASSWORD>` - replace this with the password for the user
  specified in `--username`. The template includes a database user
  for the project as the `--username`. If you want to authenticate
  as a different user, replace the value of `--username` and
  specify the password for that user in `--password`.

  If your password contains special characters, surround it in double
  or single quotes. For example, if your password is `@bc123`, you
  must surround it in quotes, such as `"@bc123"`.

- `<DATABASE>` - replace this with the name of the database you want to export data from

Include any other [mongodump reference page](database-tools/mongodump) command line options as required
before copying and pasting the command into your system terminal and
executing the full command. See [mongodump reference page](database-tools/mongodump) for complete
documentation on the available command line options and how to use
them.

> **Note:** 
>
> Connecting to an 
>
> Atlas
>
>  cluster from Microsoft Windows
>
> For users on Windows (Microsoft Windows), specify
> `mongodump.exe`
>
> You may need to provide the full file
> path to the `mongodump.exe`.

sections.heading: Connect with mongoimport
sections.body:
The Data Import and Export Tools section of the Command Line Tools tab
displays a copyable template with the minimum required options for
connecting [mongoimport reference page](database-tools/mongoimport) to your Atlas cluster.
For instructions on finding the Command Line Tools tab,
see [Access Command Line Tools](view-command-line-tools).

The template includes placeholder values for certain options.
You must replace these placeholders with the appropriate values for
your Atlas cluster:

- `<PASSWORD>` - replace this with the password for the user
  specified in `--username`. The template includes a database user
  for the project as the `--username`. If you want to authenticate
  as a different user, replace the value of `--username` and
  specify the password for that user in `--password`.

  If your password contains special characters, surround it in double
  or single quotes. For example, if your password is `@bc123`, you
  must surround it in quotes, such as `"@bc123"`.

- `<DATABASE>` - the name of the database you want to import data into.

- `<COLLECTION>` - the name of the collection you want to import data into.

- `<FILETYPE>` - the file type of the data source you are importing data from. See [--type](manual/reference/program/mongoimport) for more information.

- `<FILENAME>` - the name of the data source you are importing data from. See [--file](manual/reference/program/mongoimport) for more information.

Include any other [mongoimport reference page](database-tools/mongoimport) command line options as required
before copying and pasting the command into your system terminal and
executing the full command. See [mongoimport reference page](database-tools/mongoimport) for complete
documentation on the available command line options and how to use
them.

> **Note:** 
>
> Connecting to an 
>
> Atlas
>
>  cluster from Microsoft Windows
>
> For users on Windows (Microsoft Windows), specify
> `mongoimport.exe`
>
> You may need to provide the full file
> path to the `mongoimport.exe`.

sections.heading: Connect with mongoexport
sections.body:
The Data Import and Export Tools section of the Command Line Tools tab
displays a copyable template with the minimum required options for
connecting [mongoexport](database-tools/mongoexport) to your Atlas cluster.
For instructions on finding the Command Line Tools tab,
see [Access Command Line Tools](view-command-line-tools).

The template includes placeholder values for certain options.
You must replace these placeholders with the appropriate values for
your Atlas cluster:

- `<PASSWORD>` - replace this with the password for the user
  specified in `--username`. The template includes a database user
  for the project as the `--username`. If you want to authenticate
  as a different user, replace the value of `--username` and
  specify the password for that user in `--password`.

  If your password contains special characters, surround it in double
  or single quotes. For example, if your password is `@bc123`, you
  must surround it in quotes, such as `"@bc123"`.

- `<DATABASE>` - the name of the database you want to export data from.

- `<COLLECTION>` - the name of the collection you want to export data from.

- `<FILETYPE>` - the file type of the data source you are exporting data to. See [--type](manual/reference/program/mongoexport) for more information.

- `<FILENAME>` - the name of the data source you are exporting data to. See [--file](manual/reference/program/mongoexport) for more information.

Include any other [mongoexport](database-tools/mongoexport) command line options as required
before copying and pasting the command into your system terminal and
executing the full command. See [mongoexport](database-tools/mongoexport) for complete
documentation on the available command line options and how to use
them.

> **Note:** 
>
> Connecting to an 
>
> Atlas
>
>  cluster from Microsoft Windows
>
> For users on Windows (Microsoft Windows), specify
> `mongoexport.exe`
>
> You may need to provide the full file
> path to the `mongoexport.exe`.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.body:
If you are experiencing issues connecting to your cluster, see
Troubleshoot Connection Issues.
